verses.text: Nebuchadnezzar makes Jehoiakim a vassal, then raiders harass Judah, for the LORD would not pardon the innocent blood Manasseh had shed. The old guilt is now repaid. Sin long unrepented is not forgotten by God; its account comes due at last.

Nebuchadnezzar makes Jehoiakim a vassal, then raiders harass Judah, for the LORD would not pardon the innocent blood Manasseh had shed. The old guilt is now repaid. Sin long unrepented is not forgotten by God; its account comes due at last.

Nebuchadnezzar makes Jehoiakim a vassal, then raiders harass Judah, for the LORD would not pardon the innocent blood Manasseh had shed. The old guilt is now repaid. Sin long unrepented is not forgotten by God; its account comes due at last.

Nebuchadnezzar makes Jehoiakim a vassal, then raiders harass Judah, for the LORD would not pardon the innocent blood Manasseh had shed. The old guilt is now repaid. Sin long unrepented is not forgotten by God; its account comes due at last.

Jehoiakim dies, and Egypt no longer comes out to help, for Babylon has taken all that was Egypt's. Judah's props are knocked away one by one. When God removes a nation's defences, every ally it trusted fails.

Jehoiakim dies, and Egypt no longer comes out to help, for Babylon has taken all that was Egypt's. Judah's props are knocked away one by one. When God removes a nation's defences, every ally it trusted fails.

Jehoiakim dies, and Egypt no longer comes out to help, for Babylon has taken all that was Egypt's. Judah's props are knocked away one by one. When God removes a nation's defences, every ally it trusted fails.

Jehoiachin reigns three months and does evil like his father. The doomed line continues its pattern to the end. Even on the brink of ruin, an unrepentant house keeps walking the road that brought it there.

Jehoiachin reigns three months and does evil like his father. The doomed line continues its pattern to the end. Even on the brink of ruin, an unrepentant house keeps walking the road that brought it there.

Nebuchadnezzar's army besieges Jerusalem, and young Jehoiachin, his mother, and his servants go out and surrender. The king gives himself up to the conqueror. The judgment foretold has come to the very gates, and there is no escaping it.

Nebuchadnezzar's army besieges Jerusalem, and young Jehoiachin, his mother, and his servants go out and surrender. The king gives himself up to the conqueror. The judgment foretold has come to the very gates, and there is no escaping it.

Nebuchadnezzar's army besieges Jerusalem, and young Jehoiachin, his mother, and his servants go out and surrender. The king gives himself up to the conqueror. The judgment foretold has come to the very gates, and there is no escaping it.

Babylon strips the temple and palace of their treasures, cutting up the golden vessels, and carries away the king, the nobles, the craftsmen, and the mighty men — ten thousand captives. The glory of Jerusalem is hauled to Babylon. What pride once displayed, judgment now seizes.

Babylon strips the temple and palace of their treasures, cutting up the golden vessels, and carries away the king, the nobles, the craftsmen, and the mighty men — ten thousand captives. The glory of Jerusalem is hauled to Babylon. What pride once displayed, judgment now seizes.

Nebuchadnezzar makes Zedekiah king, but he too does evil and at last rebels against Babylon — for the LORD's anger had decreed Judah's casting out. The final king repeats the final folly. A people ripe for judgment finds even its rulers driving it toward the end.
